What are the advantages of MODAL:
- the fine fibres make modal very skin-friendly, it is often called "the softest fibre in the world"
- it is compared to viscose, however it is more durable, much more pleasant to the touch (it has a fine satin finish) soft and resilient - non-moisture
- resistant to washing, shrinking and damage
- hypoallergenic
- does not need ironing if dried properly
- does not crease as easily as viscose and modal fibres do not get damaged by hard water and detergents and therefore retain their colour

- Advantages of organic cotton:
- is free from harmful chemicals that threaten our health.
- completely safe for the skin, does not contain impurities in the form of substances considered dangerous to the human nervous system (e.g. insecticides: parathion, diazinon. It also has no residues of heavy metals, petroleum-based substances or formaldehyde, found in conventional cotton clothing)
- its fibers, which have avoided contact with strong chemicals, are more malleable, soft to the touch and better able to withstand the passage of time thanks to which it is more durable, wears out more slowly and withstands washing better
- is hypoallergenic, ideal for all allergy sufferers, safe for skin prone to irritation or prone to atopic dermatitis
- it is a material in the production of which only natural methods are used, natural pesticides are used is ecological, and at no stage of the production of organic cotton materials are used methods that would endanger ecosystems
- fair trade principles are applied during cultivation
